name: macrosilicon
description: MacroSilicon is a leading fabless semiconductor company specializing in high-performance video processing, interface conversion, and multimedia SoC solutions. Founded in 2009 and headquartered in Hefei, China, MacroSilicon delivers innovative chips for video capture, display, transmission, and processing applications.
longDescription: As an authorized MacroSilicon distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive support for MacroSilicon's extensive portfolio of video and multimedia semiconductor solutions. MacroSilicon (宏晶微) is a leading fabless semiconductor company founded in 2009, headquartered in Hefei, China, with over 1,000 employees worldwide. The company specializes in high-performance video processing chips, interface conversion ICs, and multimedia SoC solutions for consumer electronics, professional AV, automotive, and industrial applications. MacroSilicon's product portfolio includes USB video capture chips, HDMI/MIPI interface converters, video scaler and processor ICs, and multimedia SoC platforms. With strong R&D capabilities and multiple design centers across China, MacroSilicon has established itself as a key supplier in the video semiconductor market, shipping millions of units annually to leading brands worldwide.
seoTitle: MacroSilicon Distributor | Video Processing & Interface ICs | LiTong
seoDescription: Authorized MacroSilicon distributor offering USB video capture, HDMI/MIPI converters, and multimedia SoC solutions. Technical support and competitive pricing for video processing applications.

faqs.question: What are MacroSilicon's main product categories?
faqs.answer: MacroSilicon offers four main product categories: USB Video Capture Chips for streaming and recording applications, HDMI/MIPI Interface Converters for display and camera connectivity, Video Scaler and Processor ICs for resolution conversion and image enhancement, and Multimedia SoC Platforms for integrated video processing solutions. These products serve consumer electronics, professional AV, automotive, and industrial markets.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories to find the right MacroSilicon solution for your video processing needs.

faqs.question: How do I select the right USB video capture chip?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right MacroSilicon USB video capture chip depends on your input source, resolution requirements, and target application. For HDMI sources up to 1080p60, the MS2130 series offers cost-effective solutions. For 4K capture, the MS2131 supports HDMI 2.0 up to 4K30. For analog sources (CVBS/S-Video), the MS2109 provides universal compatibility. Consider USB interface type (USB 2.0 vs USB 3.0), supported formats (MJPEG, YUY2, NV12), and software compatibility when making your selection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Identify your input source and resolution requirements first, then match with the appropriate MacroSilicon capture chip series.

faqs.question: What support does LiTong provide for MacroSilicon products?
faqs.answer: As an authorized MacroSilicon distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive support including product selection guidance for video processing applications, reference design review and customization assistance, firmware and driver support, evaluation kit provision, supply chain management and inventory planning, competitive pricing and volume discounts, fast delivery from local stock. Our FAE team has extensive experience with video processing and interface conversion applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical support team for immediate assistance with your MacroSilicon product inquiries and design challenges.

faqs.question: Which MacroSilicon products are suitable for streaming applications?
faqs.answer: For streaming applications, MacroSilicon offers several optimized solutions. The MS2130 series USB 3.0 capture chips support 1080p60 with hardware encoding, ideal for game streaming and live broadcasting. The MS2131 supports 4K30 capture for high-resolution streaming. All MacroSilicon capture chips are UVC-compliant, ensuring plug-and-play compatibility with Windows, Mac, and Linux systems. They work seamlessly with OBS Studio, XSplit, and other popular streaming software.
faqs.decisionGuide: For 1080p streaming, choose MS2130 series. For 4K streaming, select MS2131. All support major streaming platforms.

faqs.question: What is the difference between MacroSilicon HDMI converter series?
faqs.answer: MacroSilicon offers multiple HDMI converter series for different applications. The MS9332 series converts HDMI to MIPI DSI for display panels, supporting up to 4K resolution. The MS9601 converts HDMI to USB for capture applications. The MS1820 is a versatile scaler that can convert between various resolutions and interfaces. For automotive applications, the MS2202 provides HDMI to LVDS conversion with extended temperature range. Choose based on your source interface, target interface, resolution requirements, and operating environment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match source and target interfaces first. Consider resolution and environmental requirements for final selection.

faqs.question: Does MacroSilicon provide reference designs and evaluation kits?
faqs.answer: Yes, MacroSilicon provides comprehensive reference designs including schematics, PCB layouts, and BOMs for all major products. Evaluation kits are available for USB capture chips, HDMI converters, and multimedia SoCs. These kits include hardware boards, sample drivers, and documentation to accelerate your development. As an authorized distributor, LiTong can provide these reference designs and evaluation kits with technical support to help you get started quickly.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request evaluation kits early in your design phase. Reference designs can significantly reduce development time.

faqs.question: What video formats and resolutions are supported?
faqs.answer: MacroSilicon products support a wide range of video formats and resolutions. For capture chips: Input resolutions from 480p to 4K (3840x2160) at 30/60fps depending on model. Output formats include MJPEG, YUY2, NV12, and I420. For converters: Various HDMI versions (1.4/2.0), MIPI DSI/CSI, LVDS, and TTL interfaces. Color depths of 8/10/12-bit. Specific capabilities vary by product - check datasheets for detailed specifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify your required resolution and format against the product datasheet. Contact FAE for compatibility confirmation.

faqs.question: Are MacroSilicon products suitable for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, MacroSilicon offers automotive-grade products qualified to AEC-Q100 standards. These include video interface converters for infotainment systems, camera interface chips for ADAS applications, and display bridge ICs for digital clusters. Automotive products feature extended temperature range (-40°C to +105°C), enhanced EMC performance, and robust reliability for harsh automotive environments. Contact LiTong for automotive product recommendations and qualification documentation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Specify automotive requirements including temperature range and EMC needs. AEC-Q100 qualified products available.
